goal: Port structural.clone_density to Structure.clones; retire kernel

summary.title: Port structural.duplication (clone_density) to Structure; retire kernel
summary.what: Adds Structure.clones() view method (Type-2 clone detection via SHA-1 leaf-type fingerprinting), CloneMember/CloneCluster/CloneReport frozen records, and slop.structure._clones helper. Rewires structural.duplication rule to consume Structure; drops legacy_v2_shim wrapper. Deletes slop/_structural/clone_density.py (297 lines). Migrates test_clone_density.py and 3 per-language clone tests; full suite 534/534.
summary.why: Fourth structural shim retirement in the post-Intent-B port sequence. clone_density operated on parsed ASTs but maintained its own per-grammar function-node and body-wrapper tables (`_FUNCTION_NODES`, `_body_node`) that duplicate Language.callable() and Language.block_types() already declared during the NPath port. Substrate-native version eliminates the duplication and lets future view-based rules share the same body-of-callable plumbing.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.clone_density to Structure.clones; retire kernel

decisions.rationale: block_types() was added during the NPath port specifically to identify body wrappers per grammar (block, statement_block, compound_statement, body_statement). The legacy kernel kept its own body_types dict — same information, duplicated. Reading it from Language keeps the substrate vocabulary as the single source of truth.

decisions.chose: Drop the kernel's files_searched summary key
decisions.rationale: Structure doesn't expose file-discovery counts at the clone-detection layer; the parse layer already counts that. Removing the key trims one test assertion but keeps the rule output focused on clone-specific information (functions_analyzed, clone_clusters, clone_fraction).
Same as legacy. Avoids recursion-limit risk on pathological function bodies. Walking with an explicit stack + reversed-children push to preserve pre-order.

review_notes: CLI smoke test on slop source surfaces 102 clone clusters — overwhelmingly the tabular-dispatch grammar classmethods (loop_nodes, decision_nodes, import_queries, is_abstract_scope) where parallel implementation IS the design pattern. Per memory: tabular dispatch is the codified convention. User has waivers for these.
Net test count dropped from 535 to 534 — one legacy-only test (test_kernel_files_searched_count) was dropped because the new substrate doesn't expose that field at this layer.

Eight shimmed rules remain after this commit: structural.sibling_calls, structural.types.escape_hatches (any_type_density), structural.stringly_typed, structural.out_parameters, and the 7 lexical rules (stutter, verbosity, cowards, hammers, tautology, slackers, confusion).

architectural_claims: Type-2 clone detection is substrate-aligned: Structure.clones() consumes the same callable records and per-grammar body-wrapper vocabulary used by every other view-native metric.
Per-grammar function-node and body-wrapper tables retire entirely (was duplicate state with the Language hierarchy).
behavioral_changes: run_clone_density signature changes from (root, rc, sc) to (structure, rc, sc).
Summary key 'files_searched' is removed (substrate exposes parse-level file counts elsewhere; clone rule's summary now centred on clone-specific metrics).
migration_notes: Custom code importing CloneEntry/CloneCluster from slop._structural.clone_density must switch to slop.structure.records.{CloneMember, CloneCluster, CloneReport}. Field rename: CloneEntry → CloneMember.
